Hillary Clinton won the New Hampshire primary, narrowly defeating Barack Obama with strong support from women and registered Democrats.

UN Secretary-General Ban Ki-moon is sending a special envoy to Myanmar amid violent clashes between security forces and protesters.

Mexico City introduces women-only buses to improve safety and comfort for female passengers.

Kuwait's emir dissolved parliament and called for early elections amid a political power struggle.

Bodies of three abducted children and their father found in Columbus, Georgia.

c6369011986d295ce197e7304e14b207dab4c0aa:
Keri Christensen, an Iraq war veteran and mother, struggles with PTSD and depression after returning home.

Arlene Samen founded One H.E.A.R.T. to reduce maternal and infant mortality in remote regions like Tibet.

Two LSU graduate students from India were found shot dead in an apartment near campus.

Danish authorities arrested three men for plotting to assassinate a cartoonist who depicted Prophet Mohammed.

f040d657ee146cd131d34aeb31d6728b4f996666:
Medical tourism is growing as patients seek affordable cosmetic surgeries abroad, often bundled with vacation packages.
Popular destinations include South Africa, Argentina, Thailand, Brazil, Costa Rica, India, and Singapore.

Scientists discovered a 1-ton rodent fossil in Uruguay, the largest rodent ever found.

Its small teeth suggest a diet of soft vegetables and fruit.

Rawalpindi police chief stopped doctors from conducting an autopsy on Benazir Bhutto, violating Pakistani law.
New video evidence suggests Bhutto was shot, contradicting the government's claim she died from a skull fracture.

Atlanta surpassed Los Angeles as the U.S. city with the most bank robberies, reporting 350 heists in one year.

Authorities expect more robberies during the holidays and urge banks to remain vigilant.

Michael Vick wrote a letter to a judge asking for leniency after his dogfighting conviction.
Vick expressed remorse, claiming he is "forever a changed man" and not the "beast" portrayed in media.

Steven Spielberg unknowingly bought a stolen Norman Rockwell painting, helping the FBI recover it.
Stolen art often ends up in legitimate collections, with less than 5% ever recovered.

Jeffrey Jamaleldine, a German citizen, was severely wounded while serving in the U.S. Army in Iraq.

Oprah Winfrey campaigned for Barack Obama, drawing over 30,000 people in South Carolina.

Delta and American Airlines canceled hundreds of flights for wire inspections on certain jets.

Wounded Marine Ty Ziegel struggled to receive fair disability benefits from the VA after severe injuries in Iraq.
Many veterans face similar challenges due to the VA's outdated disability ratings system.

Tech gadgets like the Nintendo DS Lite, Sansa Connect, and Sony T100 are highlighted as top gift ideas.
MP3 players and podcasts are increasingly used in classrooms for flexible, on-the-go learning.

Two suspected car bombs struck Algiers, targeting UN offices and government buildings, killing at least 26-76 people.

Raul Castro becomes Cuba's president, succeeding his brother Fidel after 49 years.

Paint sealant and rustproofing are often unnecessary due to modern car manufacturing improvements.

Omar bin Laden urges his father Osama to abandon violence and seek peaceful methods.

Sunni and Shiite militias in Iraq are recruiting displaced people due to inadequate government and international aid.

Iowa's central and western regions are key battlegrounds for Thursday's presidential caucuses.

Ajax drew 2-2 with Vitesse, falling five points behind league leaders PSV Eindhoven.

Queen Elizabeth inaugurated Heathrow's new $8.6 billion Terminal 5, aiming to boost the airport's global status.

Coca-Cola was created in Atlanta in 1886 and quickly expanded internationally.

A 24-year-old gang member was arrested for a Los Angeles bus stop shooting that wounded eight people.

Police are investigating the death as an accident.

Heather Mills was awarded £24.3 million in her divorce from Paul McCartney, far less than she sought.

Roger Bergendorff was arrested in Las Vegas after ricin was found in his hotel room.

Lonely Planet author Thomas Kohnstamm admits to plagiarizing and fabricating parts of travel guidebooks.

Samsung chairman Lee Kun-hee will resign after being indicted for tax evasion and breach of trust.

Indonesian court sentenced Abu Dujana, alleged Jemaah Islamiyah military chief, to 15 years in prison.

Georgia contractor sold faulty stun grenades to law enforcement, injuring three FBI agents.

His key business advice: maintain focus and discipline while sticking to a clear vision.
ffb817ce85d7c19720ebbf0b43b01d0da61e9c06:
Aruban authorities questioned Joran van der Sloot in the Netherlands about Natalee Holloway's disappearance.
A Dutch TV program aired hidden camera footage of van der Sloot discussing Holloway's death and body disposal.
Van der Sloot denied involvement, claiming he was under the influence of marijuana during the taped conversation.
Prosecutors are appealing a judge's decision not to detain van der Sloot as the investigation is reopened.
9bfd6a5beb4e8dec59ba6d805dc9349a401da51d:
U.S. Marine Staff Sgt. Tyrone L. Hadnott pleaded guilty to abusive sexual contact with a child under 16.
He was sentenced to four years of confinement; other charges, including rape and kidnapping, were dropped.
The case sparked outrage in Japan, prompting official protests and public demonstrations.
The incident reignited tensions over the U.S. military presence in Okinawa and past crimes by American personnel.
dc7f4527bf654918c7a4bb19179949ecd5982c0f:
Cristiano Ronaldo missed an early penalty as Manchester United drew 0-0 with Barcelona at Nou Camp.
Barcelona dominated possession but failed to break down United's defense or seriously test goalkeeper Van der Sar.
Lionel Messi returned for Barcelona but was substituted in the second half without scoring.
Rafael Marquez will miss the second leg after being booked, while the tie remains open for the return match at Old Trafford.
c60e77736087bc85b372a28b4724aae6dcf0b052:
FARC commander "Karina" surrendered, urging dialogue over war and calling fellow rebels to follow her example.
She cited military pressure and fears for her safety as reasons for leaving the guerrilla group.
Karina denied involvement in specific crimes but acknowledged the group's internal violence and compartmentalization.
Colombian authorities hailed her surrender as a major blow to FARC's Front 47 and encouraged more defections.
e0953e2de7cf286ac1d87a41c62e37f21b7c7564:
Al Qaeda has reestablished a safe haven in Pakistan's tribal region despite $10.5 billion in U.S. aid.
The U.S. lacks a comprehensive, integrated plan for achieving national security goals in the area.
Most U.S. aid reimbursed Pakistan for military operations, with limited results against terrorism.
Lawmakers and agencies disagree on whether an effective strategy exists for the region.
8b6f228661fd5f3170dc2c2e6b353e1d6c3859ec:
Wii Fit motivates users to exercise by making workouts fun and engaging through interactive minigames.
The system includes a sleek balance board that tracks weight, BMI, and measures balance and posture.
Wii Fit offers 48 activities across yoga, aerobics, strength training, and balance games for a varied workout.
While exercises can be done without Wii Fit, its design and gamification encourage consistent participation.
2f482e021caf07e2f07baf111c36d1587758f188:
A curfew was imposed in Jaipur after bomb attacks killed at least 63 and wounded over 200 people.
Police suspect an international terror group, possibly Harkat-ul-Jihad al-Islami, but no group has claimed responsibility.
The attacks involved bombs on bicycles and targeted crowded markets and a Hindu temple.
Authorities tightened security and questioned six suspects as investigations continued.
dc8ce4207b91a323bf6e2fbab889efeed22aa428:
A series of eight bomb blasts in Jaipur killed at least 60 and injured 150 people.
The explosions targeted crowded tourist and market areas within 12 minutes and 500 meters of each other.
Authorities labeled the incident a terrorist attack; no group has claimed responsibility yet.
India, especially Rajasthan and Maharashtra, is on high alert following the blasts.
22a9005fe99c5dd536a4f41de4eb59d4f633ef9e:
South Korean President Lee Myung-Bak pledges to donate his entire salary to the underprivileged.
He will donate his salary throughout his five-year presidential term.
Lee previously donated his mayoral salary to children of street cleaners and firefighters.
He also promised to give away his personal fortune, keeping only a retirement house.
1b00b2d2edef396855ad392f08a6e74550af569b:
Warren Buffett tops Forbes' list as the world's richest person with $62 billion.
Bill Gates drops to third place, while Carlos Slim Helu is now second richest.
Forbes lists over 1,000 billionaires globally, with rising numbers from China, India, and Russia.
A record 50 billionaires are under 40, including Facebook's Mark Zuckerberg.
9b43472077873c45e7f278418885ad028eb993e3:
Atlanta bar owner Rufus Terrill built "Bum-bot," a robot to deter drug dealers and loiterers near his bar.
The robot, equipped with a water cannon and loudspeaker, patrols the area by remote control at night.
Local daycare operators praise the robot for improving safety, but some criticize it for targeting homeless people.
Police warn that spraying water on people could be considered assault, though no charges have been filed.
281347d0e99bc099d6991a6a60e30dc13d558216:
Video shows Polish immigrant Robert Dziekanski dying after police use a Taser at Vancouver airport.
Dziekanski, who didn't speak English, became agitated after 10 hours in the arrivals area.
Bystander Paul Pritchard filmed the incident; police returned the video after legal threats.
The incident is under investigation by police, a complaints commission, and the coroner.
703d248cce913a6036347aabd2ba011a7f660bf4:
New York Governor Eliot Spitzer is linked to a high-priced prostitution ring, shocking political observers.
Spitzer publicly apologized but did not resign or directly address the allegations.
Political experts are divided on whether Spitzer can survive the scandal or if he must step down.
If Spitzer resigns, Lt. Gov. David Paterson would become New York's governor.
517b86691dda04f41123354c1b7a7bfe1290eadc:
Gordon B. Hinckley, president of the Mormon church, died at age 97 after a period of declining health.
Hinckley led the church since 1995, doubling the number of temples and expanding its global presence.
He was known for his public relations efforts and work to include nonwhites in the church.
A successor will be chosen by the Quorum of the Twelve Apostles after his funeral.
7b361feeebfb078b80831d40d2ad8640c361e049:
John McCain clinched the Republican presidential nomination after winning GOP contests in four states.
Mike Huckabee dropped out and urged Republicans to unite behind McCain for the general election.
McCain now gains a head start as Democrats Clinton and Obama continue battling for their party's nomination.
Economic issues may challenge McCain, whose strengths are national security and military experience.
7d8e196095e4561ee4f01c650101f5591fce4a8d:
Josef Fritzl imprisoned his daughter Elisabeth for 24 years in a secret cellar, fathering seven children with her.
The hidden dungeon was accessed through eight doors, including an electronic one, and expanded over time.
Three children were raised above ground under a false story, while three remained captive with Elisabeth.
The rescued family is recovering in a psychiatric clinic, adjusting to sunlight and normal life routines.
ae03390949407f55c8d8f794896af3020d180b12:
A suspected gang member confessed to kidnapping, raping, and killing 15 girls in Basra, Iraq.
Basra suffered years of militia violence, with women often targeted for "un-Islamic" behavior.
Residents are beginning to speak out as security improves and militias lose control.
Efforts to restore Basra include cleanup projects and increased security, but economic challenges remain.
4160464d3fcba547cb1664e54ce51ad06f78b773:
Barack Obama raised over $40 million from 442,000 donors in March, with 218,000 first-time contributors.
Obama's March fundraising is below his February record of $55 million but still surpasses Hillary Clinton's $20 million.
Obama's campaign has raised about $234 million, breaking the previous Democratic primary fundraising record.
Clinton's campaign downplayed the gap, saying they have enough resources for upcoming primaries.
72b396634cc46f29b5ef146a61073ee7e7fa3cdc:
Many common words are named after real people, often unfairly tarnishing their legacies.
John Duns Scotus, St. Audrey, and Charles Boycott are among those whose names became negative eponyms.
Some, like Mickey Finn and Charles Lynch, arguably earned their infamous associations.
Eponyms like "draconian" and "shrapnel" reflect the harsh or violent actions of their namesakes.
b9b09b290cdf4d74cb64684a6cae8b6832645607:
Sen. John Kerry endorsed Barack Obama for the 2008 Democratic presidential nomination in South Carolina.
Kerry called Obama a candidate who can "bring change" and unite the country.
The endorsement is seen as a setback for John Edwards, Kerry's 2004 running mate.
Obama recently gained additional endorsements from key unions and political figures.
9e49d58fa5ab7f3e4356cb61337605ac007c49ca:
Rising biofuel production is contributing to soaring global food prices and increased hunger in developing nations.
Over 100 million people risk being plunged into hunger due to higher food costs, warns the UN World Food Program.
Critics say biofuels divert land and investment from food crops, worsening food shortages and displacing communities.
Britain has announced a $900 million aid package to help address the global food crisis.
47fe0e2bba8206014fb53a7d5247efd1e316e087:
Gasoline prices hit a record high for the 16th consecutive day, averaging $3.62 per gallon.
Shell Oil's president urges increased U.S. oil production to impact global markets and lower prices.
He opposes windfall profit taxes, arguing they would slow investment and increase reliance on imports.
Uncertainty remains about how high oil and gasoline prices could go in the future.
f54f84affaebd1d86011ad31adbf9fac4754a8f2:
A 130 mph tornado struck Atlanta's historic Cabbagetown, causing random destruction to homes and buildings.
Some residents lost everything, with looting reported before owners could recover belongings.
Despite severe damage, no injuries were reported, and the community plans to rebuild.
The tornado also damaged the iconic Fulton Cotton Mill Lofts, but residents remain resilient.
bc4992871e25e6584936af456ad4b819dcc52f27:
Bryant Purvis, a "Jena 6" member, was arrested for assault after a fight at his Texas high school.
Police say the altercation does not appear racially motivated and involved accusations about car tire damage.
Purvis admitted to grabbing and choking another student, who sustained neck marks and a bruised eye.
Purvis is awaiting trial in Louisiana for a separate aggravated battery case related to the "Jena 6" incident.
271416bf0f15c52a4417339de78dcecf378b3950:
Nigerian rebel group MEND claims attacks on oil pipelines, disrupting 169,000 barrels per day of exports.
MEND requests Jimmy Carter and George Clooney to mediate in the Niger Delta conflict.
The group cites injustice and the arrest of a member as reasons for the attacks.
Shell confirms pipeline damage; Chevron denies any of its pipelines were vandalized.
0add6b43e18072837f0bef41031d6f92f740d625:
Sin Hwa Dee started in the 1970s producing soya and oyster sauces in Singapore.
The company expanded into Yu Sheng products and noodles for restaurants and catering.
In the 1990s, Sin Hwa Dee began exporting and launched retail bottled sauces under the CHNG Kee's label.
Today, they supply major clients like Singapore Airlines, Pizza Hut, and top hotels, producing over 20 tons of sauces daily.
7060e3feb685bda7b5102e1cc8e9b35da37ca2a1:
Hollywood writers ended a 100-day strike after over 92% voted to accept a new three-year deal.
The agreement covers writers' compensation for digital and new media content, a key strike issue.
Production of TV comedies and dramas will resume, but new episodes may take 4-8 weeks to air.
The Oscars ceremony will proceed as planned, now that the strike is over.
04fbd3088e8610dd4d5bea709e7cc5ee3e53710d:
Pope Benedict XVI's U.S. visit is officially non-political, but its timing impacts the 2008 election.  
The Catholic vote is crucial and divided, influencing key battleground states like Ohio and Pennsylvania.  
Both parties are actively courting Catholic voters, with dedicated advisory groups and campaign strategies.  
The pope's messages may be spun by both sides, making his political impact unpredictable.
b198f0356c0f0cb09aba4d7d5541dd7c5ce91678:
Canada's House of Commons voted to extend the Afghanistan military mission until 2011.
Extension is conditional on NATO sending at least 1,000 reinforcements to Kandahar province.
The mission has sparked controversy, with some parties demanding immediate troop withdrawal.
Supporters cite progress in Afghan schools, healthcare, and security due to Canadian troops.
0f60442453e038c6bbe3aa525bedf2a97f459e04:
Texas has the highest U.S. speed limit at 80 mph on certain rural interstates.
Speed limits vary widely across states, from 20 mph in St. Thomas to 75 mph in much of the West.
Studies show little evidence that higher rural interstate speed limits increase fatalities.
Experts argue speed limits are influenced by safety, revenue, and local political culture.
79b423b90af6b3381ebfa34a7382ca472bdb5b35:
Thousands of Zimbabweans flee economic collapse and unemployment, seeking refuge in South Africa daily.
Refugees like Mawise Gumba struggle to find work despite qualifications, relying on shelters for survival.
Johannesburg's Central Methodist Church has become a de facto refugee camp for hundreds of Zimbabweans.
Zimbabwe's crisis is blamed on hyperinflation, job losses, and government policies under President Mugabe.
3e5a3a2dddee7ebebe57c12d0ace97a24cbabd72:
Three U.S. soldiers killed and 31 wounded in two rocket attacks in Baghdad.
Clashes between U.S. troops and Mehdi Army militia left at least 20 dead in Sadr City.
Iraqi PM al-Maliki demands Mehdi Army disband or face exclusion from politics.
42 college students kidnapped and later released unharmed in northern Iraq.
c623e4914c04811d354fa137e3bf7a00980ef7d7:
Barack Obama defended himself and his wife against accusations of lacking patriotism.
He addressed criticism over not wearing a flag pin and not putting his hand over his heart.
Obama emphasized his deep gratitude to America and dismissed rumors about his background.
He argued that true patriotism is about actions, not symbols, and criticized Republican policies.
d5ef1fb6f2df20e46fb032b9e318bc8555283e78:
Six Italian men were killed in Duisburg, Germany, in a mafia-related execution-style shooting.
The killings are linked to a feud between two 'Ndrangheta clans from Calabria, Italy.
'Ndrangheta operates through tight family ties and is now more powerful than the Sicilian Mafia.
The group earns about $30 billion annually, mainly from drug trafficking and legal businesses.
e1a36c50adadc5db9ffe6fd2c5e275d522902193:
The National Trust for Historic Preservation listed 11 endangered U.S. sites reflecting key periods in American history.
Sites include Sumner Elementary, Charity Hospital, Hangar One, and California's underfunded state parks.
Threats range from development, underfunding, and environmental hazards to post-Katrina demolition plans.
The list aims to raise awareness and encourage preservation of these culturally and architecturally significant places.
bad69c9a3c7d84a4717705a3063dd7367243772b:
Panama City Beach police seek hotel security guard Shawn Wuertly for rape and attempted murder of an Alabama student.
The 18-year-old victim survived being thrown from a sixth-floor balcony and remains hospitalized with non-life-threatening injuries.
Wuertly had previously been questioned but released due to lack of evidence; he also has an outstanding warrant in Indiana.
Police are investigating procedural errors after failing to check Wuertly's criminal background before his release.
4b40ad1f0e59b139e9a1466859ee08a048ca8d2c:
Former ABC News correspondent John McWethy died in a skiing accident in Colorado at age 61.
He was known for his outstanding, objective reporting and coverage of major global conflicts.
Colleagues praised his humility, dedication to journalism, and strong sense of family and friendship.
McWethy won multiple Emmys and other prestigious awards during his nearly 30-year career at ABC.
c17a30b4c0f8f58e3afa6279a39470f65a179f6b:
High-end musical instruments like Stradivarius violins are attracting investors seeking alternatives to traditional assets.
Investment funds and syndicates help musicians access rare instruments while offering potential profits to investors.
Exclusive violins have outperformed U.S. Treasury bonds, earning about 3.5% annually since 1850.
Investors also value supporting musicians and enjoying performances on their sponsored instruments.
6f579aad2d1f601b17e01057cd4b5e4942627230:
Mexico City introduces women-only buses to improve safety and comfort for female passengers.
The service began in January and operates on four major lines, with plans to expand further.
Passengers report feeling safer and more comfortable, citing fewer incidents of harassment.
Authorities say even a single complaint of abuse justifies the new measures.
7551f8e13485bb1738c16eccad343a14a47b2903:
Milan and Fiorentina drew 1-1 at San Siro in Serie A action.
Kaka scored for Milan with a first-half penalty; Mutu equalized with a header.
Inzaghi missed a clear chance for Milan, while Kuzmanovic hit the post for Fiorentina.
Both teams now have four points from two league matches.
257d8d38863175ec9401d0532963fb71a47f0734:
Georgia Board of Pardons and Paroles commuted Samuel David Crowe's death sentence to life without parole hours before execution.
Crowe was convicted in 1988 for murdering Joseph V. Pala and admitted to the crime.
Crowe rehabilitated in prison, counseling inmates and helping others avoid drug addiction.
The victim's family was devastated by the board's decision and did not address the parole board directly.
aa596e10dfe78dd875a7e15bfd53138bc8bde471:
A British Airways Boeing 777 crash-landed short of Heathrow's runway, severely damaging its undercarriage and left wing.
Seventeen people suffered minor injuries; all 136 passengers and 16 crew were safely evacuated.
The captain reported a loss of power and possible electronics failure during the approach.
Investigations are underway, with no evidence of terrorism; the incident caused major flight delays at Heathrow.
a0dff972172125f32b79977a47b0d250be8f71e4:
Suicide attempts among U.S. soldiers have risen to over five per day since the Iraq war began.
In 2007, 2,100 soldiers attempted suicide, compared to 350 in 2002.
Lawmakers are calling for improved mental health support and suicide-prevention programs for military personnel.
Multiple deployments, relationship issues, and stigma around seeking help are key factors in rising suicide rates.
faac3a6337907b1552b7068c8701cae665c4885c:
German woman Hannelore Marianne Krause freed after 155 days as hostage in Iraq; her son remains captive.
Militants demanded Germany withdraw troops from Afghanistan for their release.
Video threats showed Krause and her son pleading for help, with militants threatening to kill them.
German officials vow continued efforts to secure the son's release.
c741b211364d761605050776064a506d24378d10:
Nearly 100 former Chilean soldiers and secret police face prosecution for covering up disappearances during Pinochet's dictatorship.
The charges relate to "Operation Colombo," which targeted opponents of Pinochet between 1974 and 1975.
Victims were illegally arrested by the Office of National Intelligence and disappeared from detention centers.
Pinochet's regime is blamed for widespread kidnappings, torture, and killings of suspected leftists; he died in 2006.
a062b8ba08369be2364a863020a28de63a3cbf86:
Barack Obama pledges a Cuba policy focused on "libertad" and freedom for the Cuban people.
He supports easing travel and remittance restrictions for Cuban-Americans.
John McCain criticizes Obama for willingness to meet with Raúl Castro and calls for maintaining the embargo.
Younger Cuban-Americans are questioning traditional GOP support, but Republican leaders believe the community remains united.

Valentino Rossi secured pole position at his home MotoGP race in Mugello, his first of the season.
This marks Rossi's 50th career pole and 40th in MotoGP.
Rossi's lap time broke the previous pole record by over 0.8 seconds.
He leads the championship by three points ahead of Dani Pedrosa and Jorge Lorenzo.
62481fda7e7b9714b994617909349550707fe8a7:
Sharon Stone apologized for suggesting China's earthquake was "karma" for its treatment of the Dalai Lama.
Her comments sparked backlash from Chinese citizens, officials, and international observers.
Christian Dior dropped Stone from its Chinese advertisements following the controversy.
Stone expressed deep regret and sadness for causing pain to earthquake victims.
13943ef2d9c3b737c9a900f0cdb32a9c39121e6f:
Rafael Nadal advanced to the French Open last 16, defeating Jarkko Nieminen despite a blistered foot.
Nadal extended his Roland Garros winning streak to 24 matches and seeks a fourth consecutive title.
Novak Djokovic and Nicolas Almagro also progressed, with Almagro defeating Andy Murray in four sets.
Other notable wins included Ernests Gulbis and France's Florent Serra moving into the next round.
0c25e0cd62589fde1b378606febbcc5a2bafc770:
Seven England players from the Champions League final are excused from the Trinidad and Tobago friendly.
Only Rio Ferdinand and Wayne Bridge from the final will join the 22-man squad.
John Terry, Wayne Rooney, and other key players are given a holiday.
The squad includes David Beckham, Steven Gerrard, and Jermain Defoe among others.
a86bc16fad35d84e988af4cc64bf14877337d47e:
Roger Von Bergendorff indicted for possession of ricin, unregistered firearms, and firearms without serial numbers.
Authorities found ricin, weapons, and an anarchist manual in his Las Vegas hotel room.
Bergendorff was hospitalized for suspected ricin poisoning before his arrest.
If convicted, he faces up to 30 years in prison.
0f937bcc8cd07bab2d5aa9a4492d3c2430063b67:
President Mugabe warned against foreign interference in Zimbabwe's upcoming election, criticizing U.S. and British involvement.
He threatened to expel the U.S. ambassador if he continued "meddling" in Zimbabwe's electoral process.
Opposition leader Tsvangirai accused Mugabe's regime of violence and vowed to contest the June 27 runoff.
Reports of kidnappings, torture, and deaths have emerged, allegedly targeting Mugabe's opponents.
a6c0617d92807f05f02d767261c45b616382e499:
Inter Milan have sacked coach Roberto Mancini after three consecutive Serie A titles.
Mancini's dismissal follows comments about leaving after a Champions League loss, though he later retracted them.
Jose Mourinho, former Chelsea manager, is expected to replace Mancini at Inter.
Mancini is now a leading candidate for the vacant Chelsea job.
4f45a0576d353fa9b5b9d1c8e3cc0e6a1cd4897b:
Chelsea Clinton faces increased media scrutiny as she campaigns for her mother's presidential bid.
She remains press-shy, refusing interviews even with child reporters, despite her growing public role.
Her campaign appearances are linked to improved performance in key states for Hillary Clinton.
The Clinton family continues to protect Chelsea from the media, sparking debate about privacy for politicians' children.
f6d43a207c2b3c48e787087b345c23d2fb1dc92f:
Police have identified a second "person of interest" in the murder of UNC student body president Eve Carson.
ATM surveillance photos show a shadowy figure in the back seat of Carson's SUV.
Authorities believe the killing was random, with no signs of sexual assault or other injuries.
A $25,000 reward is offered for information leading to an arrest in the case.
e0127487aee3a00fe164d72eb697221079518471:
Hillary Clinton intensifies attacks on Barack Obama ahead of crucial Texas and Ohio primaries.
Clinton accuses Obama of caving to special interests and watering down nuclear industry legislation.
Obama's campaign counters, highlighting Clinton's ties to Washington lobbyists and calling for less negativity.
Democratic nomination likely hinges on superdelegates, with both campaigns vying for their support.
2f3af8decce1c0a6cd22b21df96acd40b14bb62b:
Gerolsteiner will end sponsorship of their ProTour cycling team after the 2008 season due to a marketing shift.
The company has sponsored the team since 1998, investing around $12 million annually.
The German Cycling Federation will help the team search for a new sponsor.
T-Mobile fired Lorenzo Bernucci after he tested positive for a banned appetite suppressant at the Tour of Germany.
13a366f303e6ea2f07c353a9f5fb49a44840f808:
U.S. and Libya are negotiating compensation for past state-sponsored terrorist acts.
Talks aim to resolve claims from incidents like the Pan Am 103 and La Belle disco bombings.
An agreement could help Libya normalize relations and boost oil industry investment.
Both nations seek a fair, comprehensive settlement for victims and their families.
ee36a8b5db09b9e9a6d462116539ffc095d5c017:
Aerial photos reveal an uncontacted indigenous tribe in the Amazon rainforest, taken by Brazilian researchers.
The tribe was seen reacting to the plane, aiming bows skyward near their thatched communal huts.
Over 100 uncontacted tribes remain worldwide, with about half in the Amazon, facing threats from illegal logging and disease.
Advocates urge urgent protection of these tribes' territories to prevent extinction.
d3b3bcf90226a694361cb27f1a4d481c79b7368a:
Tropical Storm Arthur, the first named storm of 2008, formed near Belize and may re-enter the Gulf of Mexico.
Storm warnings issued for Belize and parts of Mexico, with up to 15 inches of rain expected in some areas.
NOAA predicts an above-normal Atlantic hurricane season with up to 16 named storms and five major hurricanes.
Half of surveyed Gulf Coast residents lack disaster plans or survival kits as hurricane season begins.
e5e837f2cc75fc44ebf826822aa4c9dc59344b8b:
Iraqi imams condemned a U.S. soldier's shooting of the Quran during Friday sermons.
The incident sparked protests and calls for severe punishment from both Sunni and Shiite leaders.
The U.S. military and President Bush apologized, but anger persisted across Iraq and Afghanistan.
The soldier was relieved of duty, reprimanded, and reassigned to the United States.
1317a869cc1d6d534a93eaad67fb6550aa155ae6:
Americans have historically criticized new immigrants for not assimilating quickly enough.
A new Manhattan Institute study shows recent immigrants assimilate faster than those a century ago.
Assimilation is measured in economic, cultural, and civic terms, with some groups excelling in different areas.
Despite fears, America continues to successfully assimilate immigrants over time.
f856ca4cd15992e0e88ae9113bf31b2a5ab55072:
Australian leg-spinner Stuart MacGill announces retirement after the second Test against West Indies.
MacGill ends his career with 207 wickets in 43 Test matches over 10 years.
He credits bowling alongside greats like McGrath and Warne for his success.
The ongoing Test in Antigua is likely to end in a draw, marking his farewell.
438e8eaa1c64cb59288f9954355d0100181d19a2:
Indonesian businessman Tung Desem Waringin dropped $10,700 from a plane to promote his new book.
The cash drop took place over a soccer field in Serang, west of Jakarta.
Police denied permission for the stunt in Jakarta due to safety concerns.
Millions in Indonesia live on less than $2 a day, making the event highly impactful.
470618f9fd48a5d134a44b5de166b625f596a0b3:
Online pharmacies are selling prescription drugs without doctor visits, leading to addiction and overdose deaths.
CNN investigation showed drugs like Prozac and Soma can be easily purchased online with minimal checks.
Authorities say the internet is fueling a major pharmaceutical abuse crisis, with millions seized in related investigations.
Efforts to regulate online drug sales face legislative indifference and enforcement challenges.
751e8f720d24c57e79cf63d9a3f72cd80c5d9619:
Democratic rivals criticized Obama for saying he'd send U.S. forces into Pakistan without approval to target terrorists.
Clinton warned Obama's remarks could destabilize Pakistan and endanger nuclear security.
Dodd argued removing Musharraf could lead to a worse, fundamentalist regime in Pakistan.
Biden noted that Obama's proposed strategy is already existing U.S. policy.
6a985a1090f146976d33c8b33342320a65a50211:
U.S. military promises action after video exposes appalling conditions in Fort Bragg barracks.
Soldiers returning from Afghanistan faced mold, rust, sewage overflows, and unsafe living conditions.
Army officials and lawmakers called the situation unacceptable and pledged immediate improvements.
Construction of new barracks is behind schedule, leaving troops in 50-year-old buildings.

EPA imposes new restrictions on rat poisons to protect children and wildlife.
Only certified professionals can buy rat poison in bulk; loose bait and hazardous types banned for personal use.
New rules require rat poisons to be sold in secure bait stations and containers meeting agency standards.
Environmental groups praise the move, citing reduced risks of poisoning and environmental justice concerns.

Suspected Islamic insurgents fired mortars at Somalia's president's plane; no injuries reported.
Al-Shaabab, an Islamic militia, claimed responsibility for the attack at Mogadishu airport.

Somalia remains unstable, with ongoing conflict between government forces and Islamic militants.
10dafcc21761c60f8fc5bd832daf8f21cf0fc66d:
Tornadoes and severe storms killed 22 people across Oklahoma, Missouri, and Georgia over the weekend.
A mother in Oklahoma died protecting her child, who survived with injuries.
Winds reached up to 175 mph, destroying homes and leaving some areas looking like a "war zone."
President Bush pledged federal support, and states declared emergencies as cleanup and recovery began.
75da85a72e0028e1b07c65f6b771a1eaa20b4e04:
A Nationwide Airlines Boeing 737 lost an engine shortly after takeoff from Cape Town, causing panic onboard.
The pilot managed to dump fuel and safely land the plane; all 100 people onboard were unharmed.
Officials believe an object was sucked into the engine, causing its catastrophic failure.
The engine had recently undergone a major overhaul and had relatively low flight hours since then.

The Tennessee Valley Authority's critical infrastructure is vulnerable to cyber attacks, a GAO report finds.
Key security flaws include weak firewalls, ineffective passwords, and inadequate virus protection.
A congressional audit questions claims that most utilities have fixed known cyber vulnerabilities.
Lawmakers may seek new federal powers to enforce cybersecurity standards for utilities.
81e50c6cdbea4e55af6307d653135ef395b6d7fe:
MREs and C rations evoke strong memories for military personnel and civilians during emergencies.
Many recall creative ways to improve or heat the meals, often using Tabasco sauce or vehicle heat.

MREs are appreciated for their practicality in tough situations, despite mixed reviews on flavor and texture.
59fa6c3439d216272cc845d35627f8eb11b08a6d:
Emmanuel Adebayor scored a second-half hat-trick as Arsenal beat Derby 6-2 at Pride Park.

Theo Walcott, Nicklas Bendtner, and Robin van Persie also scored for Arsenal.
2eff3ba44cfaefbff89ebc764828ae9e4d477c9b:
A 220-square-mile section of Antarctica's Wilkins ice shelf has collapsed, with a much larger area at risk.

The collapse does not affect sea levels but may impact local wildlife and ecosystems.
Several Antarctic ice shelves have collapsed in recent decades, signaling broader climate change impacts.

A woman died after being struck by a leaping spotted eagle ray while boating off the Florida Keys.
The ray caused multiple skull fractures and direct brain injury, according to the medical examiner.
Such accidents are extremely rare, with officials calling it a "freakish" and astonishing event.
Spotted eagle rays are not aggressive but can leap from the water, sometimes colliding with objects.

Maria Sharapova was upset by Dinara Safina in the French Open fourth round after blowing big leads.
Sharapova was booed by fans as she exited, failing to acknowledge the crowd.

Safina will face Elena Dementieva next after her emotional comeback victory.

Solar power transformed Gudda village, bringing light and new opportunities to its 100 families.

Barefoot College trains locals, especially women, in solar engineering and sustainable solutions.
Solar-powered water purification and cookers have improved health and daily life in the region.

An unauthorized biography of Cruise has also sparked debate, with both Cruise and the church disputing its accuracy.

Barack Obama becomes the first African-American to secure a major party's presidential nomination.

Obama shifts focus to general election, criticizing John McCain and praising Clinton's historic campaign.

NTSB is investigating a Boeing 757 wing panel separation on US Airways Flight 1250.

The missing wing panel has not been found; investigators are searching for it.

Joran van der Sloot was secretly recorded describing Natalee Holloway's body being dumped at sea after her 2005 disappearance.

The U.S. reinstated Fulbright scholarships for seven Gaza students after initially rescinding them due to Israeli travel restrictions.

Chinese police arrested 16 Buddhist monks in Tibet for alleged involvement in bombings in Mangkam county.
Authorities claim the suspects confessed, influenced by "separatist propaganda" from the Dalai Lama.

Hello Kitty generates over half of Sanrio's billion-dollar annual revenue.

Sanrio's founder values company reputation and societal contribution over pure profit.

President George Bush condemned Zimbabwe's government for violence and intimidation against opposition figures.

A car bomb exploded near the Danish Embassy in Islamabad, killing at least six and injuring up to 18 people.

Sen. Edward Kennedy's brain tumor surgery was successful with no expected permanent neurological damage.

Political leaders from both parties praised Kennedy's legacy and offered support during his health battle.

Hillary Clinton will suspend her presidential campaign and endorse Barack Obama for party unity.

U.S. Navy ships carrying cyclone aid for Myanmar are leaving after three weeks of denied access by the junta.

A gold cup from ancient Persia, dating to the third or fourth century B.C., sold for $100,000 at auction.

Artist Robert West draws inspiration from his grandfather, a Pullman Porter, for his train paintings.

Zimbabwe held elections amid allegations of widespread irregularities and opposition claims of fraud.

Dutch court rejects asylum plea of gay Iranian teen Mehdi Kazemi, citing EU regulations.

Jurors viewed videos of the 2005 London transit bombings and their aftermath during a conspiracy trial.

Government urges political dialogue with opposition as international observers note some election irregularities.

Air Force Chief of Staff Gen. Michael Moseley and Secretary Michael Wynne resigned over mishandling of nuclear weapons.
Incidents included a B-52 bomber unknowingly flying with nuclear missiles and accidental shipment of nuclear components to Taiwan.

Defense Secretary Gates and lawmakers emphasized the need for accountability and improved nuclear safety measures.

The U.S. Embassy in Belgrade evacuated nonessential staff after being attacked by protesters angry over Kosovo's independence.

Homeland Security will bypass environmental laws to build hundreds of miles of US-Mexico border fence.

Kerstin Fritzl, victim of the Austrian incest case, is recovering well and regaining strength after hospitalization.

Unexploded WWII German V1 bomb found at a Canary Wharf construction site in London.

46ce664a42eac4474d25c435a28790ec25137603:
President Mugabe warns war veterans may take up arms to prevent opposition from gaining power.
Mugabe accuses opposition leader Tsvangirai of wanting to return Zimbabwe to white control.

Over 600 Iowa Army National Guard soldiers returned home after 17 months in Iraq.

48e4930e3c380330b609f460f11520be0e29d5fd:
Graeme Le Saux faced persistent homophobic abuse during his football career due to false rumors about his sexuality.

He urges football authorities to lead by example and address prejudice within the game.

3695132378151f89d210da575bfef0a3f29aa051:
Queen Elizabeth celebrates her 82nd birthday with the "Trooping the Color" parade and military ceremony.

2,000 Mexican troops deployed to Juarez to combat drug-related violence.

A human rights group found medical evidence of torture in 11 former U.S. detainees from Iraq, Afghanistan, and Guantanamo.

Findings suggest these cases represent a broader pattern of abuse in U.S. military detention facilities.

Barack Obama proposed billions in spending for education, infrastructure, energy, and health care to boost the economy.

John McCain's campaign criticized Obama's plan, arguing it would slow growth and favoring lower taxes and open markets.

Slovakian referee Lubos Michel will officiate the Champions League final between Manchester United and Chelsea in Moscow.

The British embassy in Moscow will extend hours and offer a hotline to assist English fans attending the match.

Kimi Raikkonen criticized Lewis Hamilton for causing a pit-lane crash at the Canadian Grand Prix.

Hamilton apologized, calling the incident unfortunate and not intentional.
1de0cae2832cf41f49e6fad8254247c789dfabd2:
UK Foreign Office warns of increased terrorist threat in the United Arab Emirates.

Osama bin Laden plans to release a new message addressing Western nations, according to al Qaeda sources.
The message will focus on the "reasons of the struggle" and the 60th anniversary of the Israeli occupation.

Bin Laden's previous messages criticized Western involvement in Iraq and Afghanistan and the publication of controversial cartoons.

Rising Mississippi River floodwaters threaten multiple Illinois and Missouri towns, breaching levees and prompting evacuations.
National Guard, residents, and inmates work together to build makeshift levees and sandbag vulnerable areas.

Federal and state agencies are providing aid, with disaster declarations in 24 Iowa counties and recovery centers established.
dc7e18d14b8ad29abbd79f554324ec7b8ba8b9c0:
Security expert Gavin de Becker emphasizes the importance of trusting your intuition to prevent danger.

He urges people, especially women, to recognize that uneasy feelings are valid warning signs.

A woman discovered her stillborn son's body was kept in a hospital jar for four years instead of being cremated.

The hospital has apologized for the error and confirmed legal proceedings are underway.

Andre Berto won the vacant WBC welterweight title by stopping Miguel Rodriguez in the seventh round.

Khan described the bout as one of his toughest fights and aims to learn from his mistakes.
309ae632df7b88a9569d81fa91aff3b9fb03529e:
Michelle Obama is refining her public message for the general election, focusing on personal stories and her background.

Observers note her independence, confidence, and the importance of defining herself before opponents do.

Australian cricketers will honor Jane McGrath during a match against West Indies by wearing pink ribbons.

Nepalese authorities detained at least 650 Tibetan exiles protesting China's policies ahead of the Olympic torch relay.

Supreme Court rules child rapists cannot receive the death penalty, reserving it only for murder cases.

Congress overrode President Bush's veto of a $300 billion farm bill for a second time.

Luke Russert discussed youth voting, his father's legacy, and his own future in journalism on "Larry King Live."

NASA's Mars Phoenix Lander successfully touched down near Mars' north pole after a 296-day journey.

Pregnant soldier Spc. Megan Lynn Touma was found dead in a Fayetteville, North Carolina motel.

Hamas' takeover of Gaza brought relative safety but severe shortages of food, gas, and basic goods.
Israel's blockade has crippled Gaza's economy, leaving most residents dependent on UN food aid.

Opposition supporters in Zimbabwe report severe torture and violence ahead of the presidential runoff.

Hillary Clinton returns to the Senate after ending her presidential campaign, receiving a warm welcome from colleagues.

Prince William helped the U.S. Coast Guard intercept a speedboat carrying nearly a ton of cocaine.

Five European divers stranded on an Indonesian island fought off a Komodo dragon with rocks.

The Bush administration has escalated covert operations in Iran, including sending U.S. commandos to spy on nuclear sites.

About 50,000 South Koreans protested in Seoul against the government's decision to import U.S. beef.

A U.S. soldier used the Quran for target practice, sparking outrage among Iraqi leaders and citizens.

Protests erupted, with calls for accountability and concerns over repeated disrespect toward Islamic symbols.

President Cristina Fernandez defended increased grain export taxes and urged farmers to respect the law during protests.

About 6 million Chinese-made tire valve stems are being recalled by an Ohio distributor.

Simon Mann, a British mercenary, was sentenced to 34 years for plotting a coup in Equatorial Guinea.

UNC student body president Eve Carson was shot multiple times, including in the head, according to her autopsy.

Anwar Ibrahim claims to have evidence that sodomy charges against him were fabricated by senior government officials.

FDA orders "black box" warnings on certain antibiotics for risk of tendon ruptures and tendonitis.

Barack Obama and Hillary Clinton appeared together at a rally in Unity, New Hampshire.

Seventeen babies in a Texas hospital's NICU received overdoses of the blood thinner heparin.

Hurricane Bertha strengthened to a Category 3 hurricane with 120 mph winds Monday evening.

There is a small chance of U.S. landfall, but Bermuda could be affected this weekend.

Anonymous developers plan to sell software to unlock iPhones for use on networks beyond AT&T.

38649fdccec39afa649297349b2ad2e5e1a39d82:
Susan Atkins, terminally ill Manson follower, was denied compassionate release from prison.

North Korea may be willing to end its nuclear program due to economic desperation and international sanctions.

Economists warn that issuing larger notes won't help unless inflation is addressed.

Companies are offering creative incentives to help employees cope with high gas prices, such as rolling billboard cars.
Jobing.com pays employees $500 a month and free gas to drive cars wrapped in company ads.
A recent survey found 57% of companies offer commute-easing programs, including four-day workweeks, carpools, and public transit subsidies.
Yahoo rewards green commuting with perks like movie tickets and massages, while some employees switch to fuel-efficient cars on their own.

Radovan Karadzic, accused architect of Bosnia's worst war crimes, was arrested after a decade-long manhunt.
Global leaders hailed the arrest as a historic breakthrough for justice and Balkan stability.

Michael Essien signs a new five-year contract with Chelsea, extending his stay until 2013.

Marius Kloppers, born in South Africa in 1962, is the CEO of BHP Billiton.

Barack Obama and Hillary Clinton appeared together at a New York fundraiser to unite Democratic supporters, especially women.
Obama highlighted the struggles of women in his family and called for equal pay, paid leave, and economic security for women.

112bc4ab4b6e7d6dd35fc33cd946fbfc4c9356d6:
President Bush defends attending Beijing Olympics, saying a boycott would insult the Chinese people.

3b1d3309825780556178569cd40d880a793f8da7:
Check a vehicle history report before buying a used car to uncover hidden problems.
Look for red flags like many previous owners, harsh locations, or suspicious body work.

President Bush called India's prime minister to push forward a proposed U.S.-India nuclear partnership.

Photos of sunbathers near the bodies of two drowned Roma girls sparked outrage in Italy.
The girls, aged 12 and 13, drowned while swimming near Naples and their bodies were left on the sand.

The incident fueled debate over racism and discrimination against the Roma community in Italy.

Congress overrode President Bush's veto to halt a 10.6% cut in Medicare payments to doctors.

Authorities say the executions aim to deter crime and increase public security.

Young women in their 20s and 30s can also be affected and should get regular clinical exams.
Witherspoon advocates for open conversations and continued action to find a cure.
9b087c5572cc091c519625d37d1a614b97b8ed30:
Barack Obama is in excellent health, according to his longtime doctor.
Obama is an intermittent smoker but is currently using Nicorette gum successfully.
Obama released only a brief health statement, while John McCain released extensive medical records.
Both presidential candidates were described by their doctors as being in excellent health.
b5211115ff68e12e759a474a02d3b163211af563:
U.S.-led forces captured two senior al Qaeda in Iraq leaders in Baghdad in August.
One suspect, Abu Uthman, allegedly masterminded the 2006 kidnapping of journalist Jill Carroll.
Both men are linked to car and suicide bombings aimed at inciting sectarian violence.
Their capture removes two experienced leaders from the al Qaeda in Iraq network.
6b75abea2de5c9c9952af0427ccec78910ac811f:
A man has been arrested for the murder of Auburn University freshman Lauren Burk.
Courtney Larrell Lockhart faces multiple capital murder charges related to kidnapping, robbery, and attempted rape.
Burk was found shot and her car set on fire near campus; police are investigating all leads.
A campus-wide memorial service for Burk is scheduled, and donations can be made to her favorite charities.
259cc1db3d4c814dafc02ed071327f65a7b9eaad:
Panic and long lines reported at gas stations across multiple states ahead of Hurricane Ike's landfall.
Some stations imposed limits or ran out of regular unleaded as demand surged.
Gas prices spiked in some areas, with rumors fueling further panic buying.
Police were called to maintain order at overcrowded stations in certain locations.
3bb1cda1e01cb6e14f6190ced3b1ec3f7ef50de9:
Coalition troops handed over security control of Iraq's Anbar province to Iraqi forces.
Anbar was once a hub of Sunni insurgency and al-Qaeda activity.
U.S. troops will remain to support Iraqi forces as needed.
Anbar is the first Sunni-dominated province to revert to local control.
7afbe903f1f7cdc1af509583649babf481d01729:
Robert Rogers lost his wife and four children in a 2003 Kansas flash flood.
He survived and became a minister, sharing his story to inspire hope and faith.
Rogers established a ministry and orphanages worldwide in honor of his lost family.
He encourages others to cherish family and live life with no regrets.
79d625a3311f4ff11598a3ae4fca63103e84e9a1:
29 Russian doomsday cult members, including 4 children, are barricaded in a cave awaiting the world's end in May.
Cult leader Father Pyotr Kuznetsov is in police custody and undergoing psychiatric evaluation.
Authorities are concerned for the children's safety as cult members threaten suicide if forced out.
Russian Orthodox officials warn of dangers from lack of proper religious education.
ea6c4267f17d55f1bd9f0950c31ef59565bdc30b:
Six people were killed and 13 wounded in South Ossetia after Georgian shelling, officials say.
Georgia and South Ossetia blame each other for the violence, with conflicting reports on who started the attack.
Civilians are being evacuated from the conflict zone amid fears of continued military operations.
Russia urges both sides to seek diplomatic solutions and warns against escalation.
851d02b689bec14607574096bb77b9acf53c15ee:
Zimbabwe's inflation rate has soared to 11.2 million percent, the highest in the world.
Prices for basic goods, like bread, have skyrocketed despite government price controls.
Analysts say the real inflation rate may be even higher, exceeding 20 million percent.
The economic crisis has led to shortages, mass emigration, and failed political negotiations.
f2d00c8c396698becc18fd2b41ba595c7a3e1cc6:
A columnist humorously describes her frustration with a mysterious coworker, "The Tinkler," who leaves messes in the office bathroom.  
Colleagues become amateur detectives, speculating about the culprit's identity and motives.  
The situation highlights the quirks and irritations of office life and shared spaces.  
The author ultimately longs for more civility and cleanliness in the workplace.
70c6ca3234f9b1221c8aea7671dd78e1ca9d9139:
The Wildland Firefighter Foundation has provided over $1.5 million in aid to more than 500 families since 1999.  
Founder Vicki Minor offers emergency assistance, emotional support, and advocacy for injured and fallen wildfire fighters' families.  
The foundation helps with lodging, travel, and financial needs, especially when official benefits are delayed or denied.  
A monument in Boise, Idaho, honors fallen wildland firefighters and provides a place for families to grieve together.
86eaa90c419d5ed93341248fecd8c25d820f2b82:
The New York Giants' win over the Patriots is hailed as one of the biggest Super Bowl upsets.
David Tyree's remarkable catch and Eli Manning's performance are highlighted as game-changing moments.
Fans from across the country and world share emotional and memorable reactions to the game.
Many consider Super Bowl XLII the best ever, with celebrations and pride especially strong in New York.
a1841cf61c53937fffc54c0e81d8f7cfcad2f0c2:
Deadly Hindu-Christian riots continue in Orissa, India, with about 20 deaths reported and violence spreading to Karnataka.
A police officer was killed when a Hindu mob torched a police station in retaliation for arrests.
Attacks on churches and Christian communities have led to over 140 arrests, including both Hindus and Christians.
Tensions began after the killing of a Hindu leader, with both sides blaming each other and past violence fueling fears.
5d221c86fdadca378eeaabc39c881cc74efcff07:
Hillary Clinton's campaign apologized for Bill Clinton's harsh language toward a Vanity Fair article.
The article suggested Bill Clinton's behavior changed after heart surgery and cited anonymous sources about alleged affairs.
Bill Clinton called the reporter a "scumbag" and criticized the use of unnamed sources.
Vanity Fair's Todd Purdum defended his reporting, while Clinton's team called the article unfair and inaccurate.
7a49f5bc1d336309f57076bceba91885ee5368b5:
Prince William begins a two-month attachment with the Royal Navy for military experience.
He will train in sea survival, firefighting, and ship damage repair before joining HMS Iron Duke.
William previously completed attachments with the Royal Air Force and British Army.
The training prepares him for his future role as head of the armed forces when he becomes king.
dd46893fd318dce4e209f9a335612659ebf8fd24:
Parma sign 18-year-old Alberto Paloschi from AC Milan in a co-ownership deal.
Paloschi scored on his Milan debut but faces limited playing time after new signings.
Parma were relegated from Serie A last season and seek to strengthen their squad.
Milan's Massimo Oddo may leave for Lyon, losing his spot to Gianluca Zambrotta.
acda8749fba5e9553e9f47cef89ef85eae5ad4e3:
JetBlue flight from Boston to Fort Lauderdale diverted after fight over smoking in bathroom.
Plane landed in Raleigh-Durham, North Carolina; one passenger taken into custody.
Fight involved three related individuals, leaving one person injured in the face.
Passengers were held for two hours while FBI conducted interviews.
20b11518df6520f552d691d5f6c0d02755d11809:
Army Staff Sgt. Darris J. Dawson and Sgt. Wesley Durbin were killed by another U.S. soldier in Iraq.
Dawson's family says the military has provided almost no information about his death.
The military classified the deaths as "non-hostile," causing confusion and frustration for the family.
Critics allege the military downplays combat casualties to minimize public perception of war costs.
51fe083191bb5568bf6a5fea1610a8e19663d7b2:
New York Governor orders state agencies to recognize same-sex marriages from other states and countries.
The directive follows a state appeals court ruling requiring recognition of legal same-sex marriages performed elsewhere.
Agencies must revise policies to comply with New York's human rights law.
California and Massachusetts are among the states whose same-sex marriages will be recognized in New York.
8af047c2d83a91e8b745adfcaa7c282dfe1030a2:
Hurricane Ike's aftermath leaves Texas residents struggling with destroyed homes and uncertain futures.
Evacuees in Austin and San Antonio face hardships, relying on shelters and donations for basic needs.
Power outages and curfews in Ohio spark frustration and strict measures to prevent crime.
Some families, unable to return home, are relocating permanently to new communities.
dfcacd955a9168b2cf599c57a539dbfce43ea2a9:
Susana Trimarco has spent years searching for her daughter, a victim of human trafficking in Argentina.
Her story inspired the hit Argentine soap opera "Stolen Lives," raising awareness about human trafficking.
Trimarco's foundation has rescued 360 women and children and helped push for anti-trafficking laws.
Despite threats, Trimarco continues her activism and educates the public while raising her granddaughter.
f2463954bc7215d8a1f98cf0d85f280fd2affa58:
A 6.1 magnitude earthquake struck southern Iceland, causing injuries and significant damage to roads and buildings.
The epicenter was about 31 miles from Reykjavik and felt nationwide, prompting evacuations and emergency response.
No fatalities were reported, but 15 to 20 people near the epicenter required medical treatment.
Most homes withstood the quake, but some hospital wings and infrastructure were damaged and closed.
0e7a92a6f06e94cdd613860d4a3f4b92a06a2b43:
An American sailor shot and killed two female sailors before shooting himself at a Bahrain military barracks.
The alleged shooter survived and is in critical condition under heavy security at a military hospital.
The Navy stated the incident was not terror-related and involved only U.S. military personnel.
The motive for the shootings remains unclear, and the base was briefly closed after the incident.
2905a15c5947042e42f4b52dc201d24822af20f1:
Over 100 countries adopted a treaty in Dublin to ban cluster bombs and their explosive bomblets.
The treaty requires immediate destruction of stockpiles and cleanup of contaminated areas.
Major producers like the US, Russia, China, and Israel did not sign the accord.
Cluster bombs often leave unexploded bomblets, posing long-term risks to civilians in affected areas.
808a3d788317af05686ee71a71d2fbc5c19267bc:
Indonesia will withdraw from OPEC at the end of the year due to declining oil production.
The country has become a net oil importer, importing more than it exports since 2006.
Indonesia's oil production has dropped 32% since 1996, falling below its OPEC quota.
Rejoining OPEC is possible if Indonesia's oil production recovers in the future.
5a1bec30205b6938392f3119366e27d15b4849e8:
Rear Adm. John Stufflebeem was demoted and fired for lying about a sexual affair in the White House.
He falsely told the woman he was a widower and later denied the affair to investigators.
The woman, "Jane Doe," confirmed the relationship, and her testimony was supported by supervisors.
Stufflebeem was convicted of making false official statements and requested retirement after the hearing.
7ff39e1220c0f084434786ed145e15c6abe48def:
Two U.S. customs agents at JFK Airport were arrested for helping smuggle drugs and contraband.
Agents allegedly accepted bribes to allow illegal drugs and counterfeit goods through airport security.
A sting operation led to the indictment of over 20 people and the seizure of 600 pounds of drugs.
Counterfeit luxury watches and designer sunglasses were among the smuggled items.
7551503e7e57bd519913b0df90ca1e80d5305b05:
Guatemala and Vietnam have halted international adoptions amid concerns of corruption and child trafficking.
Thousands of U.S. families are left in limbo, with pending adoptions under case-by-case review.
New safeguards, including DNA tests and in-person verification, are being implemented to prevent fraud.
The suspension affects two of the top ten countries for U.S. international adoptions, causing emotional and financial distress.
c34a0cbf6db61323766fc86b1b89b399ff6b0666:
Three men re-arrested in connection with Natalee Holloway's 2005 disappearance in Aruba due to new evidence.
Joran Van der Sloot and brothers Deepak and Satish Kalpoe now face manslaughter-related charges.
Dutch and Aruban authorities are collaborating on the renewed investigation; suspects maintain their innocence.
Holloway's family remains hopeful as the case sees new developments after years of stalled progress.
d07a61368a195444b14bd4b382e0fc3d64890b22:
Free Burma Coalition founder criticizes China and India for not pressuring Myanmar to accept more cyclone aid.
ASEAN also accused of failing to help cyclone victims effectively.
Myanmar's military rulers blamed for prioritizing their own security over disaster response.
Official Cyclone Nargis death toll exceeds 40,000, with fears it could be much higher.
aa68dee4bc246e5aae9507342f8520a2a301a310:
Xenophobic violence in South Africa has killed over 40 people and displaced about 28,000, mainly targeting immigrants.
Clashes spread from Johannesburg to Cape Town, resulting in more deaths, injuries, and mass evacuations of foreign nationals.
Mozambique declared a state of emergency after 10,000 of its citizens fled South Africa due to the violence.
South African President deployed the army to help police, while aid workers provide shelter and supplies to affected foreigners.
87ef15064035db73cb29f5cec2f0442f2c527ef0:
Former Illinois Gov. George Ryan will remain in prison after the Supreme Court refused to hear his appeal.
Ryan and co-defendant Larry Warner were convicted of racketeering and fraud related to state license bribes.
Their lawyers argued the trial was unfair due to juror removal, but appeals courts upheld the convictions.
Ryan is serving a 6½-year sentence and is known for halting Illinois executions in 2000.
a577b934e210ef7dce07390711f03db8bb2e005a:
Rebel group MEND claimed responsibility for sabotaging a Shell oil pipeline in Nigeria's Rivers State.
MEND said it killed 11 government soldiers in a clash after the attack, but the military denied this.
The attacks have reduced Nigeria's oil production by about 10%, impacting global oil prices.
MEND seeks a greater share of oil wealth for the impoverished Niger Delta region.
c9414bf55859cb9ca7e925a4a09b416b6f66446a:
Top authors recommend their favorite summer reads for quick escapes and long weekends.
Suggestions include classics, thrillers, poetry, and feel-good novels for every reading mood.
Books are grouped by one-day reads, long weekend reads, summer-long savors, and dip-in-and-out options.
Selections range from "The Great Gatsby" to "One Hundred Years of Solitude" and "The Portable Dorothy Parker."
f12cda150b96acfa1499b2afb0a81029545abadf:
Barack Obama received key endorsements from former Clinton Labor Secretary Robert Reich and ex-Senators Sam Nunn and David Boren.
Reich, a longtime Clinton friend, praised Obama's policy proposals as stronger than Hillary Clinton's.
Nunn and Boren will advise Obama's campaign on national security issues.
The endorsements come just days before the crucial Pennsylvania primary.
7359741b0e60b4402f069963a26c7aa766f45e7c:
Researchers have reprogrammed human skin cells to act like embryonic stem cells, sparking debate on ethical research.
President Bush and supporters claim the breakthrough validates restrictions on embryonic stem cell research.
Opponents argue embryonic stem cell research should continue, as it may still offer unique medical benefits.
Scientists emphasize that both reprogrammed and embryonic stem cells are important for future research.
b10c71f49dbeabad23325d625e8e7b8529429607:
Josef Fritzl confessed to imprisoning and raping his daughter for 24 years, fathering seven children with her.
Fritzl vacationed in Thailand while his daughter remained trapped in the cellar beneath his house.
Family members, including Elisabeth and her children, have had an "astonishing" reunion after their rescue.
DNA tests confirmed Fritzl as the father; authorities say his wife was unaware of the crimes.
c5a1e04f5f84d406ffedfc2f312c314d1bc03c46:
Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah pledged cooperation in Lebanon's political process, rejecting dominance or imposition.
Michel Sleiman was elected president after a six-month vacancy, following a power-sharing agreement brokered in Qatar.
The new government structure gives Hezbollah-led opposition veto power in the Cabinet, ending an 18-month political crisis.
Nasrallah emphasized national unity and healing divisions, promising inclusive opposition representation in the new government.
8a5035d3527c4db4357dd1db9fb2712008dccea9:
A Texas appeals court ruled the state had no right to remove FLDS children from their parents.
FLDS parents say their children are suffering and feel betrayed while in state custody.
A CNN legal analyst predicts the children will likely be returned to their families if the ruling stands.
Texas Child Protective Services is considering its next steps and maintains its duty to protect children.
3f33d4cb25cd121619ba99eeb02709b72716d622:
Consumer Reports found that cheaper cars can cost more to own long-term than pricier alternatives.
Depreciation is the largest ownership cost, followed by fuel, interest, and insurance.
Maintenance and repairs are a smaller portion of costs, but vary significantly by brand and model.
Hybrids like the Toyota Prius can be cheaper to own over five years than similar conventional cars.
e6f451d6667f142a64818a9d05dbc49115e7cbf9:
Presidential candidates are not "regular folks" but members of the elite, with privileged backgrounds and wealth.
Both Democratic and Republican leaders have attended elite schools and enjoy lifelong benefits and high salaries.
Attempts by politicians to appear relatable are superficial and do not reflect their true lifestyles.
Media figures criticizing "elitists" are often wealthy and privileged themselves, perpetuating the same myth.
39622c01625247af942a6439699ff529db1d54d0:
Carmen Salva leads Esperanza de Vida, a youth group cleaning up northern Argentina's mountains every Saturday.
The group collects and recycles trash, teaching children environmental responsibility and the importance of caring for nature.
Participation has grown to about 150 people, including 80 children, with support from local schools and government.
Children are inspiring their parents and community to adopt better environmental practices for future generations.
02927c88776bcc84fc883435a5037b4c8ca70e83:
Sixty-four measles cases reported in the U.S. this year, the highest in seven years.
All but one case involved unvaccinated individuals; some were too young for vaccination.
Most cases were imported from other countries, especially Europe and Israel.
Fourteen patients were hospitalized, but no deaths have been reported.
3ee31d8987c07ba4ea9423d39c5e333525316958:
Amnesty International's annual report highlights global human rights abuses, focusing on China, the US, and Russia.
China faces criticism for repressing activists, minorities, and free speech, especially ahead of the Olympics.
The US is scrutinized for Guantanamo detentions, the death penalty, and leadership failures on human rights.
Other concerns include violence in Iraq and Afghanistan, political repression in Russia, and worsening conditions in Zimbabwe.
f8ddc6d36f378757d27a244529f8fe11672baa41:
Judge declares mistrial in retrial of six men accused of plotting terrorist acts with al Qaeda.
Jury deliberated for 13 days without reaching a verdict; this is the second mistrial in the case.
Defendants, known as the "Liberty City 7," allegedly targeted the Sears Tower and FBI offices.
Unclear if government will pursue a third trial; a seventh defendant was previously acquitted.
4e97228ceef1be54ab9151fc6759017e6a54a18d:
U.S. Border Patrol agent Luis Aguilar was killed by a suspected drug smuggler's vehicle near Andrade, California.
Aguilar was deploying spike strips when struck; the suspects fled back into Mexico.
Authorities from both countries are cooperating to apprehend those responsible.
Aguilar is survived by his wife, two children, and a brother who is also a border patrol agent.
0e649ccabaf0e650e9757ee256c2749d37486ba6:
Rozita Swinton is a person of interest in phone calls that led to the Texas polygamist ranch raid.
Swinton, 33, was arrested in Colorado for false reporting, unrelated to the Texas case.
Authorities suspect Swinton may have impersonated a 16-year-old girl alleging abuse at the ranch.
The raid resulted in 416 children being removed, and officials are still trying to identify the caller.
9eccfcc231c7c0d65cb0c333cb3d1731a5fe2f3f:
Denzel Washington urges leaders to address the crisis facing America's youth, including high dropout and obesity rates.
He credits Boys & Girls Clubs for positively shaping his life and advocates for supporting such organizations.
Washington calls on citizens to volunteer, donate, and hold politicians accountable for prioritizing children's futures.
He emphasizes that everyone can play a role in helping youth realize their potential and become future leaders.
ddf109b3de6022a4ecd62ee2cd31d2c2ac51035b:
Thousands protested China's human rights record and Tibet crackdown as the Olympic torch arrived in San Francisco.
Protests followed similar demonstrations in London and Paris, disrupting the torch relay and leading to arrests.
The Olympic flame was kept at an undisclosed location ahead of its only U.S. relay appearance.
Some IOC members suggest ending the global torch relay early due to ongoing disruptions and public relations issues.
bd92eab23374d95ac4430e2e1a8bf7561c662309:
Jockey Frankie Dettori and chef Marco Pierre White co-founded Frankie's restaurants after struggling to find family-friendly dining.
Their restaurant chain has expanded to London, Shanghai, and Dubai since 2004.
Dettori released "Frankie Dettori's Italian Family Cookbook" featuring his favorite childhood recipe, Aubergine Parmigiana.
The recipe is vegetarian, evokes family memories, and serves eight with a three-hour preparation time.
d84440effc748f9220c7dde068962e803f566bc7:
Middlesbrough have released Fabio Rochemback, Gaizka Mendieta, and Dong Gook Lee from their squad.
Manager Gareth Southgate opted not to extend Rochemback's contract, praising his recent performances.
Goalkeeper Mark Schwarzer is in contract talks, with his future at the club still uncertain.
Tottenham's Jamie O'Hara signed a new three-year deal, while Radek Cerny joined Queens Park Rangers.
e2866f596c85b0191ce812260d23855c70a01192:
Birmingham City co-owner and managing director were interviewed by police in a corruption probe.
The investigation focuses on payments to a football agent and two players from 2002-03.
No allegations suggest directors or the club benefited financially; the issue concerns PAYE and National Insurance.
The club is fully cooperating with police, and share trading was temporarily suspended during the probe.
4ebd9195bc8c12272b6281a5a610a7b5fb49b8cd:
Jaguars, once thought extinct in the U.S., have been photographed and tracked in Arizona near the Mexico border.
Conservationists fear the border fence will block jaguar migration and threaten their survival in the region.
Environmental groups are challenging Homeland Security's waivers of environmental laws to expedite fence construction.
Wildlife researchers say the fence impacts both animal movement and their ability to study endangered species like the jaguar.
4bd37aa7004cd1fdf1ccb506d2159a2cf26995e0:
Sudan and Chad signed a non-aggression pact to halt cross-border hostilities after two days of talks.
Both nations accuse each other of supporting rebel groups that destabilize their governments.
The UN and Senegalese President facilitated the agreement, aiming to normalize relations and stop rebel movements.
Previous peace agreements have failed, and the region faces a growing refugee crisis due to ongoing violence.
29b87dc549b8c64b35517e684e79b1da6316bd88:
International arms dealer Viktor Bout charged with conspiring to sell weapons to Colombia's FARC rebels.
Bout allegedly agreed to provide missiles, rocket launchers, and drones to attack U.S. targets in Colombia.
He faces four terrorism-related charges and is currently in custody in Thailand, pending extradition.
U.S. officials call Bout one of the world's most prolific arms traffickers; his U.S. assets were frozen in 2004.
d2f2fe0b8797b416bae71305c9d1c874e2fdd0cc:
Hillary Clinton's chief strategist, Mark Penn, is stepping down after controversy over his firm's meeting with Colombian officials.
Penn's firm discussed a U.S.-Colombia free-trade deal, which Clinton opposes, leading to criticism and his resignation as strategist.
Penn will continue to advise Clinton's campaign but will no longer serve as chief strategist.
The Colombian government fired Penn's firm after his remarks, calling them disrespectful.
0269d39abbea3edadd225fa97d818f5a789b4c72:
Thousands protested China's human rights record and Tibet crackdown as the Olympic torch arrived in San Francisco.
Protests followed similar demonstrations in London and Paris, disrupting the torch relay and leading to arrests.
The IOC will discuss the torch relay amid calls to end the global tour early, but no decision is on the agenda.
Some torchbearers withdrew from the relay due to safety concerns, and security remains tight around the event.
394f20c9bee9bc8d0e6a65bac48de57bd9f4733d:
Skybus Airlines is shutting down, becoming the third airline this week to cease operations.
Rising jet fuel costs and a slowing economy were cited as reasons for Skybus's closure.
ATA Airlines and Aloha Airlines also announced shutdowns this week amid bankruptcy filings.
Passengers with future Skybus reservations are advised to seek refunds from their credit card companies.
6e63c8b1ae01ab91b4832222edaf4bb461b6c568:
A U.S. sailor was arrested in Japan for the robbery and murder of a Japanese taxi driver.
The suspect, Olatunbosun Ogbogu, was handed over by the U.S. military to Japanese authorities.
Recent crimes involving U.S. service members have fueled controversy and anti-American sentiment in Japan.
U.S. officials pledged cooperation with Japanese authorities to ensure justice is served.
355875856932fd634aaf7c6b26155403aaf15904:
"Street Kings" follows Keanu Reeves as a rule-breaking, troubled LAPD detective entangled in corruption and betrayal.  
The film sticks closely to classic Cop Noir tropes, offering little originality in its storytelling.  
Forest Whitaker energizes the cast as Reeves' mentor, while Hugh Laurie plays the Internal Affairs investigator.  
Despite strong performances, the movie is criticized for its predictable plot and lack of deeper insight.
999523913d281ab57977d79b644f3879ac4d2e37:
Texas authorities are investigating child safety at a polygamist sect's ranch led by Warren Jeffs.
About 400 followers live at the 1,900-acre YFZ Ranch near Eldorado, Texas.
Social workers and police entered the compound after a referral, but no safety issues have been confirmed yet.
Warren Jeffs, the sect leader, is currently jailed on charges related to sexual conduct with minors.
5f7424604ec11358dc6fdfa47732f90422949041:
Mikhail Gorbachev said Georgia provoked the conflict in South Ossetia, not Russia.
Gorbachev described Georgia's attack on Tskhinvali as a "barbaric assault" using sophisticated weapons.
Georgian President Saakashvili criticized Gorbachev for defending Russia and called his statements "lies and deceptions."
Gorbachev warned that U.S. support for Georgia could harm U.S.-Russia relations.
fdb2b65b9cb99b7d52559ae647cae293cb5910d3:
At least 19 schoolgirls died in a dormitory fire at Buddo Junior School near Kampala, Uganda.
Two adults' bodies were also found in the ashes after the blaze.
Survivors reported hearing an explosion before the fire started.
Conflicting reports emerged about whether the dormitory doors were locked, trapping the children inside.
f974fe1b3777ffc5756322c0bb3f31a9b23ec09a:
U.S. officials suspect Pakistan's ISI aided the July bombing of India's Embassy in Afghanistan.
Evidence includes intercepted communications between Pakistani intelligence and militants, but is not conclusive.
Pakistan strongly denies involvement, calling the allegations unfounded and malicious.
Afghanistan and India have previously accused Pakistan of supporting militants in the region.
2930e1a7ac24bce649bfbf5f5971e74515e38a0a:
Gloria Taylor, mother of murdered schoolboy Damilola Taylor, died of a suspected heart attack in London.
She and her husband founded the Damilola Taylor Trust to help underprivileged youth after their son's death.
Damilola Taylor was killed in 2000, prompting national debate on inner-city issues and youth violence.
Two brothers were convicted of Damilola's manslaughter in 2006 after multiple trials and investigations.
f5f7571a69e96c1d288d939861b7216c2bdc0ae4:
Pope Benedict XVI arrived in the U.S. for a six-day visit, greeted by President Bush at Andrews Air Force Base.
Security is extremely tight, with 27 agencies involved and precautions at major events like Mass at Nationals Park.
The pope aims to energize U.S. Catholics and address issues like the sex abuse scandal and interfaith relations.
Benedict will also visit New York, address the U.N., and celebrate Mass at Yankee Stadium.
5f02aa32bd1dc95e47355755398e31550b232f8a:
Eight Florida teens, ages 14-18, will be tried as adults for a videotaped beating of a 16-year-old.
The attack, allegedly retaliation for online insults, left the victim hospitalized with serious injuries.
Authorities say the teens planned to post the video on YouTube to humiliate the victim.
Experts warn that posting violent "shock videos" online is an increasing trend among teens.
9177e5ac94f038749e8d4eb526a65461e0f6df4c:
The judge found Heather Mills exaggerated her wealth and career success before marrying Paul McCartney.
Mills' claim of being McCartney's "business partner" was dismissed as make-belief by the court.
McCartney was found to be generous, but did not give Mills 30 valuable paintings as she claimed.
The court determined McCartney's wealth at £400 million, rejecting Mills' higher estimate and her expectation to maintain his lifestyle.
f12e4bbb07211de7d43b4e331dc73404aa804562:
Sarah Knox Taylor Davis, daughter of Zachary Taylor, married Jefferson Davis but died of malaria three months later.
Elizabeth Harrison Walker, Benjamin Harrison's daughter, became a lawyer and economic commentator, active on radio and TV.
Margaret Woodrow Wilson, Woodrow Wilson's daughter, was a singer and later lived in an Indian ashram until her death.
Elizabeth Ann Christian Blaesing, Warren Harding's daughter by his mistress, lived quietly after a scandalous early life.
Margaret Truman Daniel
